title: Quantification of Fungal ITS Gene Copies Using ddPCR
author: Eva Petrova

steps.title: All reagencies must be equilibrated to...
steps.instruction: All reagencies must be equilibrated to RT (do not keep them on ice). Mix each of them properly before use.

steps.title: %s %s Prepare the master mix...
steps.instruction: %s %s Prepare the master mix according to the number of samples and mix several seconds by vortexing. Transfer mix into 96-well plate à 20 µl. 20 µL, master mix per well ddPCR 96-well plates Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #12001925 QX200™ ddPCR™ EvaGreen Supermix Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1864033 Tip: use a mechanical or electronic dispenser (for ex. Multipette, Pipettman, or multichanel pipette) during this step to speed up the work.

steps.title: Add 2 µl of your DNA...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µl of your DNA sample into each well 2 µL, of examined DNA per well

steps.title: Seal the plate (180°C, 5s) with pierceable aluminium foil
steps.instruction: Seal the plate (180°C, 5s) with pierceable aluminium foil. PCR Plate Heat Seal foil piercable Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1814040 00:00:05, sealing at 180°C

steps.title: Let the foil cool down and...
steps.instruction: Let the foil cool down and mix the plate vigorously by vortexing 30 s - 1 min. 00:00:30, vortexing

steps.title: Put all the staff (cartridges, tips,...
steps.instruction: Put all the staff (cartridges, tips, sealed plate with samples and empty 96-well plate in cooling stand) in corresponding amount inside the AutoDG machine (Bio-Rad). Per one sample there is a need of two pipette tips! This is an example how it should look like inside the AutoDG before strating droplets generation.

steps.title: Make sure there is a right...
steps.instruction: Make sure there is a right oil bottle ( Automated Droplet Generation oil for Probes ) connected to the system. Automated Droplet Generation Oil for EvaGreen Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1864112

steps.title: Choose a position of samples on...
steps.instruction: Choose a position of samples on touch screen and start dropplets generation. Wait after its finished. System will you announce automatically about success or failure of droplets creation after procedure is finished. Nevertheless, every time make also a visual inspection of droplets. Two separated phases should be visible. Upper part with dropplets and lower clear oil phase. selection of a columns within the plate where the samples are positioned This is how it should look like inside the AutoDG after droplets generation. plate after droplets generation - two phases visible in each well with sample

steps.title: Take the plate with droplets out...
steps.instruction: Take the plate with droplets out of the machine and seal it with pierceable aluminium foil (170°C, 3s). PCR Plate Heat Seal foil piercable Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1814040 00:00:03, sealing at 170°C

steps.title: Put immediately the sealed plate into PCR cycler
steps.instruction: Put immediately the sealed plate into PCR cycler. Droplets are unstable at this stage. Proceed to next step as fast as possible. After PCR droplets become stable and can be kept at fridge for some time (one day) before measurement.

steps.title: Clean AutoDG machine, waste used consumables
steps.instruction: Clean AutoDG machine, waste used consumables.

1. 95 ◦ C –5′ 2. x 40 { a. 95 ◦ C – 30′′ b. 52 ◦ C – 2′ } 3. 4 ◦ C – 5′ 4. 90 ◦ C –5′ 5. 10 ◦ C –hold Set ramp rate for each step to 2°C/sec! Set reaction volume to 40ul. 40 µL, reaction volume After run is finished check if there are still two phases present Let the plate cool down (droplets will not be so sticky and will be more easy to analyse).

steps.title: Put the plate into a metal...
steps.instruction: Put the plate into a metal holder, place them together into QX200 reader . Switch on the reader 30 min before measurement. Droplet reader with a plate after PCR already placed inside the metal holder

steps.title: Set up the QuntaSoft experiment as follows
steps.instruction: Set up the QuntaSoft experiment as follows: Exp. type: Absolute quantification (ABS) Supermix: QX200 ddPCR EvaGreen Supermix Target1: Ch1 (for FAM labeled probes) Define the position of ech sample.

steps.title: Check the levels of reader Oil...
steps.instruction: Check the levels of reader Oil and waste - green control (bottles are physically accessible from machines left side). If the instrument was not in use longer than week it has to be Primed first (oil flushed).

steps.title: Start measurement
steps.instruction: Start measurement. After it finishes go over the results and see how many droplets were executed for each sample. To get reliable results total droplet count should be above 12.000. We usually have got 18.000 - 20.000 droplets analyzed per sample. an example of total droplets count (positive together with negative ones) being generated and read

steps.title: Set up the treshold just above...
steps.instruction: Set up the treshold just above negative control sample in order to distinguish positive (containing PCR products) from negative (do not contain any PCR product) droplets. Quanta software will automaticaly calculate copy number of target gene for each sample by Poisson distribution algorithm. For that calculations at least some of the negative droplets are necessary. If the sample contains only positive population it can not be evaluated. An example of ITS Fungi copy numbers data analysis. Last two samples on the left graph are negative (NTC) samples by which a treshold was set up.

steps.title: Export
steps.instruction: Export .csv file with concentrations (copies/ul) that are posessing you the number of copies in 1ul of reaction. To obtain number of copies in 1ul of your input DNA you have to recalculate: no. of copies in 1ul of input DNA = (concentration value x 22)/ volume of DNA
